text: never have existed at all . The pain came roaring back when the voice was silent, and she suffered alone. There were other voices, distant, muffled, and unfamiliar, and she shrunk from them. They came often, sometimes alone, sometimes in groups . Time had no meaning. She didn’t have a name, didn’t have a memory. But she had that voice to cling to. That voice to anchor her, the keep her from spiraling away into oblivion. It was the only thing to ease her pain, the only thing to look forward to . Don’t leave yet . She didn’t want to leave him yet. Not yet. Not even when the pain reached its worst, and the other voices grew louder, and the darkness thickened impossibly . Don’t leave yet . The words echoed through the void and she grasped at them. She didn’t want to be taken away, she refused . Don’t leave yet . Lara opened her eyes, and it was the hardest thing she’d ever done. Vision blurred, she battled her heavy eyelids and stared into blinding white light. Something beeped to her right, slow and rhythmic. She blinked; the room came into focus slowly, and the shapes didn’t make sense. The unfamiliar voices were clear , now . “How long is this going to take?” A man asked from nearby . “I can’t say for sure,” a woman replied. “She’ll wake up when her mind and body are ready .” “It’s been three days, and she hasn’t so much as wiggled a finger .” Focusing all her will through her confusion and pain, Lara twitched a finger . “We can’t just force her to wake up, Dave. That’s not how it works .” “She might never wake up at all, Nancy .” “That’s a possibility. But I’m not — ” “Every moment we keep her here is a drain on our resources. Those machines draw power from a limited pool, and nobody’s making new medical supplies anymore. She’s an outsider, and she’s taking up the time of our medical staff .” “She’s used hardly anything so far, and she’s a human being . Isn’t a life still worth more than a few plastic tubes and bit of my time?” Nancy demanded quietly . “And if she doesn’t come out of it? The cost only adds up over days and weeks. She’s going to need to be fed, and that’ll cause more complications. We can’t allow this to go on indefinitely .” “That’s not your call to make, Dave. And you’re stupid if you think her companion will let you cut her off .” “He couldn’t lay a hand on me,” Dave said uncertainly . “You think rules will stop him ?” Who were they? Where was she ? Lara blinked again, and dragged her gaze around the room. The blinding white dissolved into differing shades — the ceiling, the cloth, the walls, the sheet draped over her legs, all subtly varied whites. Nothing real could be so pure, so clean . The nearby beeping continued, picking up speed and volume. She parted her dry lips and winced as they split. Sliding out her tongue, she tasted blood. When she lifted her right arm, something tugged at it, producing a sharp pain in the back of her hand. Her left arm was wrapped up tight and slung to her neck. When she tried to move it, a bone-deep ache throbbed up to her shoulder . She turned her head, following the tubes and wires that ran from her arm up to the machines near the bed . What the fuck ? “She’s awake!” Nancy exclaimed. “Go get Ronin !” People crowded around the bed, their features obscured by shadow as their bodies blocked the light. They all seemed to speak at once, rapidly, using words Lara didn’t understand . The beeping continued to gain speed, and her chest hurt, each breath more labored than the last. She lifted her right hand, moving it closer to her left. She needed to pull the foreign things from her body. Needed to get away from these people, needed air … “Hold her down, before she hurts herself !” Hands fell upon Lara and pressed her onto the bed . “Lara,” Nancy said, “my name is — oh, no, don’t !” Lara yanked her arm. There was a moment of resistance, a flare of pain, and then the wires pulled free. The beeping became a monotone, ceaseless drone . She couldn’t breathe. Her lungs were on fire . The hands closed around her arms and legs, dragging her down. Lara fought them. Agony swept through her body . Warlord stood above her, fist swinging toward her face, raining blow after blow . Lara screamed. She thrashed, battling the strangers’ holds. They’d deactivated Ronin, took him from her, and Warlord kept hitting her, over and over, and the pain wouldn’t end. Her throat was raw from her screams, but the hands wouldn’t relent . “Lara, I’m Nancy, I’ve been — ” Lara’s right arm slipped from their hold and she swung her fist. Nancy cried out, stumbling back, blood oozing from her lip. Hands clamped down on Lara’s forearm and forced it down onto the bed . “Are you okay?” someone asked . “I’m fine,” Nancy replied. “We need her calmed down before she does herself more damage .” “What’s happening ? Lara ?” Lara stilled at the sound of that voice. Eyes wide, she turned her head to look between the strangers, finally catching sight of Ronin as he approached. He was here. She wanted to leap at him, to throw her arms around him, but even if she weren’t restrained, she wasn’t sure she had the strength . “Ronin,” she sobbed . The strangers shifted aside as he fell onto his knees beside the bed. He gently cradled her cheeks in his hands, spreading warmth through her face . “I’m here,” he said. “ You’re safe .” The restraining hands lifted
